metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `AnalyzerResult.loc` is a scalar (e.g. `int | None`) or a nested object with a `.max_loc` attribute; if scalar, change the AC to `returns result.loc` (not `.loc.max_loc`); if nested, update the prerequisite ticket 3b30 to define the `loc` sub-type so the contract is consistent across both tickets.
evidence: Plan AC: 'a reader function accepts an `AnalyzerResult` and returns its `.loc.max_loc`'
Prerequisite 3b30 AC: '`AnalyzerResult` (fields loc/complexity/duplication, each optional=None when unsupported)' — `loc` described as a plain field, no nested structure
Existing codebase: `max_loc` appears only as a flat integer key in `git_metrics.py` dicts, not as a sub-attribute of any `loc` object
ABSENCE: no `LocResult`, `LocData`, or any nested loc-type is defined or referenced anywhere in the repo or the prerequisite spec
findings.finding: The AC specifies `result.loc.max_loc`, treating `loc` as a nested object with a `.max_loc` sub-attribute, but the prerequisite (3b30-2168-ba16-41fc) defines `AnalyzerResult.loc` only as a plain optional field (scalar or None), with no sub-structure documented. If `loc` is a scalar, `result.loc.max_loc` raises AttributeError at runtime, breaking the stated mechanism.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria: `returns its .loc.max_loc`; prerequisite ticket 3b30-2168-ba16-41fc AC: `AnalyzerResult (fields loc/complexity/duplication, each optional=None when unsupported)`
scenarios: If `loc` is an `int` (scalar line count), `result.loc.max_loc` raises AttributeError at runtime.
If `loc` is `None` (unsupported), `result.loc.max_loc` raises AttributeError — `None` has no `.max_loc`.
Test `test_reads_loc` will fail unless the implementer invents a nested `loc` type not specified upstream, silently diverging from the prerequisite contract.
findings.suggested_fix: If `loc` represents a raw line-count integer, change the AC to: 'a reader function accepts an `AnalyzerResult` and returns its `.loc` value (the raw line count).' If a richer `loc` sub-object is genuinely needed, add a `LocMetrics(max_loc: int, ...)` dataclass to the prerequisite's `AnalyzerResult` definition and reference it explicitly here.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC clause (and a corresponding PROVE test case) specifying what the reader function returns when `AnalyzerResult.loc is None` (e.g., return `None`, raise, or return `Unavailable`).
evidence: Prerequisite ticket 3b30 AC: 'fields loc/complexity/duplication, each optional=None when unsupported'
Plan AC has no mention of None/.loc-absent handling
No test case or PROVE command exercises the None path
findings.finding: The AC specifies `returns its .loc.max_loc` but provides no fallback behavior for the explicitly allowed case where `.loc is None` (the prerequisite requires `loc` be optional=None when unsupported). The reader function's contract under a None `.loc` is fully unspecified.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria — `a reader function accepts an AnalyzerResult and returns its .loc.max_loc`
scenarios: An analyzer that doesn't support LOC returns `AnalyzerResult(loc=None, ...)`. The reader crashes with AttributeError on `None.max_loc` unless a guard is specified.
Downstream consumer receives an unhandled exception instead of a sentinel value like Unavailable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and explicitly document the sub-structure of `AnalyzerResult.loc`: if it is a plain scalar, change the AC to `result.loc` (not `result.loc.max_loc`); if it must be a structured object with `.max_loc`, update the prerequisite ticket's AC and file_impact to define that nested type.
evidence: Prerequisite ticket AC: 'AnalyzerResult (fields loc/complexity/duplication, each optional=None when unsupported)' — no nested structure or sub-attributes on `loc` specified.
Codebase search for `max_loc` returns only `src/rebar/metrics/git_metrics.py` (a top-level dict key), not any `AnalyzerResult.loc.max_loc` path.
Codebase search for `AnalyzerResult` returns no matches — the type does not yet exist in the repo.
Plan states 'only reads its `.loc` field' but AC implies `.loc` is an object: `result.loc.max_loc`.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `AnalyzerResult.loc.max_loc` — treating `.loc` as a structured sub-object with a `.max_loc` attribute — but the cited prerequisite ticket [rebar:3b30-2168-ba16-41fc] defines `loc` only as a plain field ('fields loc/complexity/duplication, each optional=None when unsupported') with no sub-attributes specified. No codebase or prerequisite evidence establishes that `loc` is a nested object with `.max_loc`; the existing `max_loc` in the repo is an unrelated top-level dict key in `git_metrics`.
findings.location: Plan body ('this ticket only reads its `.loc` field') + AC ('returns its `.loc.max_loc`')
scenarios: If the prerequisite ships `AnalyzerResult.loc` as a scalar (int or None), calling `.loc.max_loc` raises `AttributeError` at runtime and the AC test fails.
The contract mismatch between the two tickets may go unnoticed until integration, causing rework on one or both sides.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) align with the prerequisite by changing the AC PROVE target to `result.loc` (scalar int), or (b) coordinate with the prerequisite author to add a `LocResult(max_loc: int, ...)` dataclass to `src/rebar/metrics/analyzer.py` and update both tickets' ACs accordingly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify and document the type/shape of `AnalyzerResult.loc`: if it is a nested object, define the `LocResult` (or equivalent) dataclass with a `max_loc` field in ticket 3b30 or here; if it is a scalar, update the AC to match (e.g., return `result.loc` directly).
evidence: Prerequisite ticket 3b30 AC: 'AnalyzerResult (fields loc/complexity/duplication, each optional=None when unsupported)' — loc is a single field, no sub-fields defined.
The plan's AC says 'returns its `.loc.max_loc`', implying loc is a nested object with a max_loc attribute, but no such structure is established by the prerequisite.
Existing repo max_loc usage (git_metrics.py:114) is an unrelated dict key in a distribution object, not an AnalyzerResult sub-field.
findings.finding: The plan states the reader returns `result.loc.max_loc`, but the prerequisite ticket (3b30) defines `AnalyzerResult.loc` as a plain optional field without specifying it has a `.max_loc` sub-attribute. The nested shape `.loc.max_loc` is ungrounded in the established protocol.
findings.location: Plan body: 'this ticket only reads its `.loc` field' / AC: 'returns its `.loc.max_loc`'
scenarios: Implementer creates a reader that calls result.loc.max_loc, but AnalyzerResult.loc is actually an int or a dict — AttributeError at runtime and the test cannot pass.
The prerequisite ticket author implements AnalyzerResult.loc as a scalar int (matching its name), making .loc.max_loc a type error.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) update the prerequisite ticket 3b30 to define `AnalyzerResult.loc` as a sub-object (e.g., `LocResult(max_loc: int | None, ...)`) and cite that here, or (b) change the AC to `returns its .loc` if loc is the scalar max-line-count value.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and document the concrete type of `AnalyzerResult.loc` (e.g. a `LocResult` dataclass with `max_loc: int`, not a plain scalar) — either by referencing where 3b30 specifies this sub-structure, or by adding the field-type contract to this plan before the AC test is written.
evidence: AC line: 'a reader function accepts an `AnalyzerResult` and returns its `.loc.max_loc`'
Prerequisite ticket 3b30 AC: '`AnalyzerResult` (fields loc/complexity/duplication, each optional=None when unsupported)' — `loc`'s internal structure is unspecified
Existing prior art in `src/rebar/metrics/git_metrics.py` line 114: `'max_loc': max_loc` — `max_loc` is a flat dict key, not a sub-object attribute
ABSENCE: no definition of a `loc` sub-object type (e.g. `LocResult`, `LocData`) in either ticket
findings.finding: The plan's AC asserts `result.loc.max_loc` as the access path, treating `AnalyzerResult.loc` as a structured sub-object with a `max_loc` attribute — but the prerequisite ticket (3b30) only declares `loc` as a top-level optional field of `AnalyzerResult` without specifying its type. The existing codebase exposes `max_loc` as a flat `dict` key in `git_metrics.module_size_distribution()`, so `loc` could reasonably be a plain int or a richer object. This is an unverified capability assertion about the shape/type of `AnalyzerResult.loc`.
scenarios: If `AnalyzerResult.loc` is defined as a plain `int` (lines of code scalar) in 3b30's implementation, then `.loc.max_loc` is a runtime `AttributeError` and the AC test will fail.
If implementors of 3b30 and eval-266e work independently, they may choose incompatible shapes for `loc` with no contract to align on.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note to the plan (or raise a clarification on ticket 3b30) that `AnalyzerResult.loc` must be a structured type (e.g. `@dataclass class LocResult: max_loc: int | None`). This aligns with the existing `max_loc` key in `git_metrics` and makes the two-level access `result.loc.max_loc` unambiguous.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the concrete type/shape of `AnalyzerResult.loc` in the prerequisite ticket (3b30) or in this plan — specifically whether it is a dataclass/TypedDict with a `max_loc` sub-field, and update the AC to match.
evidence: Prerequisite ticket 3b30 AC: "`AnalyzerResult` (fields loc/complexity/duplication, each optional=None when unsupported)" — no sub-type declared for loc

findings.finding: The AC access path `result.loc.max_loc` treats `AnalyzerResult.loc` as a structured sub-object with a `.max_loc` attribute, but the prerequisite ticket (3b30) defines `.loc` only as a plain optional field (type unspecified, may be None). The inner schema of `.loc` — specifically that it is itself an object with a `max_loc` sub-field — is never defined anywhere in the prerequisite or in this plan.
scenarios: An implementer reads the prerequisite, creates `AnalyzerResult.loc` as a plain int or scalar (e.g., a line count), and `.loc.max_loc` raises AttributeError
Implementer guesses `.loc` is a dict and uses `result.loc['max_loc']`, which also mismatches the dotted AC notation
findings.suggested_fix: In the prerequisite ticket (3b30), expand the `AnalyzerResult` schema to declare `loc` as e.g. `Optional[LocResult]` where `LocResult` is a dataclass/TypedDict with at least a `max_loc: int` field. Then update this plan's AC to reflect that concrete type.
